description: Global skill activation rules for Claude Code. Global rules should cover process discipline and routing only. Domain-specific guidance belongs in local project skill packs.

architecture.local_layer: Domain guidance, guardrails, and project-specific workflows
architecture.principle: Do not duplicate domain guardrails in the global layer

using-superpowers.description: Entry skill for deciding whether another skill applies before acting.

skill-router.description: Route the session to the correct project directory and tell the user where to start the local pack.

intentPatterns: (where should i start|which repo should i use)

playwright-interactive.description: Use for persistent browser-based UI debugging and iterative Playwright interaction.

gh-fix-ci.description: Use when debugging failing GitHub Actions or PR checks.

gh-address-comments.description: Use when addressing GitHub PR review or issue comments with gh.

brainstorming.description: Use before creating features, adding functionality, or changing behavior.

intentPatterns: (how should we|what is the best way to).*?(build|design|structure)

writing-plans.description: Use when requirements are known and a multi-step implementation plan is needed before coding.

executing-plans.description: Use when executing an existing written implementation plan.

systematic-debugging.description: Use for bugs, failing tests, regressions, and unexpected behavior before proposing fixes.

test-driven-development.description: Use when implementing or changing code after requirements are already clear.

verification-before-completion.description: Use before claiming something is done, fixed, or passing.

high-agency.description: Use for long, high-ownership, quality-sensitive tasks.

pua.description: Escalation skill for repeated failure, passivity, or shallow retries.

intentPatterns: (try harder|do not give up|another approach|keep going)
(why is this still not working|you failed again)

global_rules_only: Do not place frontend, backend, data, research, or other domain-specific guardrails here.
Do not use file path triggers for domain code at the global layer.
Use local project skill packs for domain-specific skills and enforcement.
routing_policy: When a task is clearly domain-specific, route first, then load local skills.
When a task is cross-domain, identify a primary domain and optionally one secondary domain.
